proposed_solution: AI agent that auto-fills prior authorization forms by extracting clinical data from EHR notes, matching to payer-specific requirements, submitting electronically, and tracking status. Handles follow-ups and responds to payer information requests autonomously.
target_customer: Healthcare practice managers and revenue cycle directors at mid-size physician groups and hospitals
competition_notes: Cohere Health, Olive AI (acquired), Infinitus Health, CoverMyMeds. Market is active but fragmented; CMS 2027 API mandate creates tailwind for new entrants.
notes: Massive $23-31B annual pain. CMS FHIR API mandate by 2027 creates standardized integration points. Key moat: depth of payer-specific rules engine and EHR integrations.

proposed_solution: AI agent that triages denied claims, identifies root cause from denial codes and claim data, auto-corrects coding/documentation errors, drafts appeal letters with supporting clinical evidence, and resubmits. Escalates complex cases to human reviewers.
target_customer: Hospital CFOs, revenue cycle directors, billing company owners
competition_notes: Waystar, Experian Health, nThrive. Most incumbents are workflow tools, not autonomous agents. Opportunity for AI-native approach that handles the full denial lifecycle.
notes: RCM market is $344B globally. Denial management is 'most unwieldy area in patient financial services.' Contingency pricing (% of recovered revenue) aligns incentives and lowers adoption barrier.

proposed_solution: AI agent that investigates AML alerts by automatically gathering transaction context, customer history, and external data; assesses true risk; drafts investigation narratives; and recommends disposition. Reduces false-positive review time by 70-80%.
target_customer: BSA/AML compliance officers at mid-size banks, credit unions, and fintechs
competition_notes: Featurespace, Quantexa, Lucinity, SAS. Most offer better detection but few focus on the investigation/narrative workflow. Opportunity in the analyst copilot layer.
notes: 90%+ false positive rates mean most analyst time is wasted. SAR narrative writing is highly LLM-suited. Regulatory sensitivity requires human-in-loop but agent handles 80% of investigation legwork.

proposed_solution: AI agent that ingests invoices from any format (paper, PDF, email), extracts key data via OCR/LLM, matches to POs and receipts, flags discrepancies, routes for approval, and posts to accounting system. Handles exceptions autonomously.
target_customer: AP managers, controllers, and CFOs at mid-market companies (100-5000 employees)
competition_notes: Bill.com, Tipalti, Stampli, Coupa. Crowded market but most are workflow tools with basic OCR. AI-native approach with LLM understanding of invoice context and exception handling is differentiated.
notes: 97% of B2B invoices still processed manually. $5-15 per invoice cost. Opportunity to undercut incumbents with AI-first approach. Mid-market is underserved -- enterprise has Coupa, SMB has Bill.com.

proposed_solution: AI agent that analyzes PA denial reasons, pulls relevant clinical documentation from patient records, drafts tailored appeal letters matching payer-specific criteria, and manages the appeal submission and tracking workflow.
target_customer: Practice managers, PA specialists at physician groups and specialty practices
competition_notes: Overlap with PA submission tools (opportunity #1). Could be a module within a broader PA solution. Infinitus and Cohere partially address this but appeals remain largely manual.
notes: Natural extension of PA submission agent (opp #1). Could bundle as a full PA lifecycle platform. Appeals success directly translates to revenue recovery -- strong ROI story.

proposed_solution: AI copilot for CDI specialists that reads clinical documentation, identifies gaps in physician notes (missing specificity, unaddressed comorbidities), auto-generates physician queries with suggested language, and tracks query response rates. Improves DRG accuracy and reimbursement.
target_customer: CDI program directors, HIM directors at hospitals and health systems
competition_notes: 3M (360 Encompass), Optum (CAC), Nuance/Microsoft (DAX). Large incumbents but most focus on coding assist not CDI query workflow. Opportunity in the query generation and physician communication layer.
notes: $1.5M+ revenue lift for hospitals with strong CDI. Value-based care expansion makes CDI increasingly critical. Natural LLM application -- reading clinical text and identifying documentation gaps.

proposed_solution: AI agent that ingests contracts (PDF, Word, scanned), extracts key clauses and terms, identifies risks and deviations from standard templates, generates redline suggestions, and produces summary reports. Focused on small/mid-size firms who can't afford enterprise CLM.
revenue_model: Per-contract fee or monthly subscription tiered by volume
target_customer: Managing partners at small law firms (5-50 attorneys), in-house legal teams at mid-market companies
competition_notes: Ironclad, Juro, ContractPodAi, Luminance. Enterprise CLM is crowded but expensive ($50K+/year). Opportunity for AI-native tool at SMB price point ($500-2000/month) that does 80% of what enterprise tools do.
notes: 42% of enterprises cite risk assessment as top CLM driver. Small firms do this manually. AI contract review is a proven LLM use case. Key differentiation: price point and ease of adoption vs enterprise tools.

proposed_solution: AI agent that automates title searches by accessing digitized county records, tracing chain of title, identifying liens/easements/encumbrances, and generating title abstracts. Works across jurisdictions by adapting to county-specific record formats.
revenue_model: Per-search fee ($50-200 per search vs $200-500 manual) or monthly subscription for title companies
target_customer: Title company operations managers, real estate attorneys, mortgage lenders
competition_notes: SoftPro, Qualia, DataTrace. Most are workflow tools not AI search agents. Opportunity for LLM-powered document analysis that reads and interprets recorded instruments across varied county formats.
notes: Title insurance is $20B+ US market. Every real estate transaction requires a title search. AI can dramatically reduce abstractor time per search. Key challenge: accessing county records (varies widely in digitization).

proposed_solution: AI agent for customs brokers that auto-classifies goods using Harmonized Tariff Schedule, determines applicable duties and agency requirements, prepares entry documentation, and flags compliance risks. Learns from broker corrections to improve over time.
revenue_model: Per-entry fee or monthly SaaS subscription
target_customer: Customs brokerage firms, freight forwarders, importers with in-house customs operations
competition_notes: Descartes, Integration Point (acquired by Thomson Reuters), C4T. Most focus on trade management not classification. Opportunity for AI classification engine that handles the 10,000+ tariff codes intelligently.
notes: US imports ~$3T/year, each shipment needs classification. Misclassification penalties are severe. LLM + product description matching is natural fit. Customs brokers shortage creates market pull.

proposed_solution: AI copilot for insurance underwriters that gathers data from multiple sources (applications, loss runs, financial statements, third-party databases), synthesizes risk profiles, and recommends pricing. Reduces data gathering from hours to minutes per submission.
target_customer: Commercial insurance underwriting managers, MGAs, carrier CUOs
competition_notes: Planck, Groundspeed, Federato. Emerging market with strong VC interest. Opportunity in vertical-specific underwriting (e.g., construction, restaurants) where domain knowledge is key differentiator.
notes: Commercial insurance premiums are $800B+ globally. Underwriters handle 50-100+ submissions/month. Data gathering is 40-60% of underwriter time. Vertical specialization is the defensible moat.
